content: Ad-hoc codesigning is a checksum-only signature with no identity verification. Ad-hoc signed executables historically only run without prompting on the machine that built them; when copied/transferred to another Mac (or quarantined via download), Gatekeeper still blocks first launch and shows 'cannot be opened because the developer cannot be verified.' Older macOS versions allowed right-click->Open to bypass; project-level STACK.md research claims this no longer works as of macOS 15.1+ and the only path is System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Open Anyway. These two claims partially conflict and should be hands-on verified on the actual build/target machine early in this phase.
